# XT.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the XT REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, USDT-M Futures, Coin-M Futures, public market data, private account/trading endpoints, WebSocket streams, client-side rate limiting, local order books, dependency injection, native AOT, and CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis.

XT.Net is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem. Current package version in source: 4.1.0. This branch targets CryptoExchange.Net 12.0.0-beta1. REST and Shared REST methods use `HttpResult<T>` / `HttpResult`; socket and Shared socket subscriptions use `WebSocketResult<UpdateSubscription>`. Targets `netstandard2.0`, `netstandard2.1`, `net8.0`, `net9.0`, and `net10.0`.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, basic usage, supported functionality, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/XT.Net/): Full generated API reference and implementation docs
- [Claude/Agent Skill](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Core XT.Net patterns, pitfalls, client setup, REST/WebSocket examples
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/docs/ai-api-map.md): Table-style intent-to-method map for XT REST, sockets, and SharedApis
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed routing guide, examples, configuration, and anti-hallucination checks

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, compilable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Public ticker, authenticated balances, spot limit order, order status, cleanup
- [Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): USDT-M leverage, market order, positions, close-all pattern
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Spot ticker/order book/trades and token/listen-key private stream patterns
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): SharedApis for exchange-agnostic REST and socket code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): `HttpResult<T>`, retry decisions, symbol validation, order precision guidance

## Core Routing

- REST public spot: `new XTRestClient().SpotApi.ExchangeData`
- REST spot account/trading: `client.SpotApi.Account`, `client.SpotApi.Trading`
- REST USDT-M futures: `client.UsdtFuturesApi.ExchangeData`, `.Account`, `.Trading`
- REST Coin-M futures: `client.CoinFuturesApi.ExchangeData`, `.Account`, `.Trading`
- WebSocket spot: `new XTSocketClient().SpotApi`
- WebSocket futures: `new XTSocketClient().FuturesApi`
- Credentials: `new XTCredentials("API_KEY", "API_SECRET")`
- DI: `services.AddXT(options => { ... })`
- SharedApis: `client.SpotApi.SharedClient`, `client.UsdtFuturesApi.SharedClient`, `client.CoinFuturesApi.SharedClient`, `socketClient.SpotApi.SharedClient`, `socketClient.FuturesApi.SharedClient`
- Shared capability discovery: `client.SpotApi.SharedClient.Discover()` and equivalent SharedClient roots

## Critical Rules

- Never use raw `HttpClient` for XT endpoint calls.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- Use `XTCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`.
- Spot private socket streams require `GetWebsocketTokenAsync`.
- Futures private socket streams require `GetListenKeyAsync`.
- XT REST futures are split into `UsdtFuturesApi` and `CoinFuturesApi`; XT socket futures are under `FuturesApi`.
- Inspect `XT.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**` before generating a method not present in the map.

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base abstractions used by XT.Net and other exchange libraries
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package for unified access to all exchange libraries
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/XT.Net): Latest stable release on NuGet
- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/JKorf/XT.Net/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
